title: MCS Savanna Temple

ingest_warnings: 1.17+ downsample applied to 8 block ids: minecraft:mangrove_door->minecraft:dark_oak_door, minecraft:short_grass->minecraft:grass, minecraft:waxed_exposed_chiseled_copper->minecraft:orange_terracotta, minecraft:waxed_exposed_copper->minecraft:orange_terracotta, minecraft:waxed_exposed_copper_trapdoor->minecraft:iron_trapdoor, minecraft:waxed_exposed_cut_copper_slab->minecraft:smooth_red_sandstone_slab, minecraft:waxed_weathered_copper->minecraft:lime_terracotta, minecraft:waxed_weathered_copper_grate->minecraft:iron_bars

description:
This Chinese-style temple is a roughly rectangular structure, approximately 19x14 cells at its base, rising to 21 cells high, featuring a single storey. The most striking feature is its elaborate hip roof, which has a multi-tiered, slightly upturned profile, characteristic of East Asian architecture. The roof is predominantly constructed from dark brown oak planks, with a diagonal checkerboard pattern of lighter spruce and birch planks, creating a decorative tiled effect. The walls beneath the eave overhangs are made of smooth sandstone and spruce planks, giving a warm, earthy tone. The building is surrounded by a simple perimeter of stone brick slabs and grass, with scattered red and yellow accent blocks suggesting a path or decorative elements.

The interior of this temple is likely a single, open space, given its one-storey design. The main entrance appears to be on the longer side of the structure, indicated by a dark opening. While specific rooms are not visible, the design suggests a central hall suitable for ceremonial or communal gatherings, typical of a temple. The lack of visible windows implies a dimly lit interior, relying on the main entrance for light or internal light sources not depicted. The overall impression is one of a modest but culturally significant structure, designed for a specific spiritual or community purpose within a traditional Chinese architectural context.
